title: EP20: Yann LeCun
excerpts:
Section Title: EP20: Yann LeCun > **Yann LeCun – Why LLMs Will Never Get Us to AGI** > **Transcript**
Content:
**00:00:00 – Introduction**
**Ravid Shwartz-Ziv:** Hi, Yann. And welcome to The Information Bottleneck. And I have to say, this is a bit weird for me. Like I've known you for almost five years and we've worked closely together, but this is the first time that I'm interviewing you for a podcast, right? Usually our conversations are more like, "Yann, it doesn't work, what should I do?" Okay, so even though I'm sure all of our audience knows you, I will say Yann LeCun is a Turing Award winner, one of the godfathers of deep learning, the inventor of convolutional neural networks, founder of Meta's Fundamental AI Research Lab, and still their Chief AI Scientist and a professor at NYU. So welcome.
**Yann LeCun:** Pleasure to be here. Yeah.

title: Yann LeCun: the godfather of m… - Economist Podcasts - Apple Podcasts
excerpts:
Section Title: Yann LeCun: the godfather of machine learning is building “a new revolution in AI”
Content:
The launch of R1, an AI model by the Chinese startup DeepSeek, recently sent shockwaves through the technology world. R1 is a “reasoning” model—the most cutting-edge type of large language model (LLM)—and it performs about as well as the best-in-class Western models but for a fraction of the training cost. Like other LLMs, though, it still lacks many of the skills and types of intelligence that human brains achieve. For one, “reasoning” models still have a very limited understanding of the physical world in which they exist. Our guest today wants to get beyond these hurdles. Yann LeCun, chief AI scientist at Meta and a professor at New York University, thinks LLMs are not the answer if we want truly useful personal assistants, humanoid robots and driverless cars in the future.
